mission.title: argo-workflows: getting `empty git-upload-pack given` from wf using git artifacts as input
mission.description: getting `empty git-upload-pack given` from wf using git artifacts as input. Requested by 27+ users.

steps.title: Check current argo-workflows deployment
steps.description:
Verify your argo-workflows version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo-workflows -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-workflows
helm list -n argo-workflows 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working argo-workflows installation.

steps.title: Review argo-workflows configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant argo-workflows configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n argo-workflows -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-workflows
kubectl get configmap -n argo-workflows -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=argo-workflows
```
Workflows using git artifacts as input fail with `empty git-upload-pack given` error. The pipeline stays stuck at the init stage while trying to clone the git repository.

steps.title: Apply the fix for getting `empty git-upload-pack given` from wf using git…
steps.description:
This bug was caused after the commit https://github.com/go-git/go-git/commit/320db9af8ba8b0046e833013504eb07c61a3573c on the [go-git](https://github.com/go-git/go-git) upstream. This commit was supposed to allow us to fetch a new depth on a shallow clone (more details here
```yaml
artifacts:
          - name: argo-source
            path: /workspace
            git:
              repo: https://myuser@bitbucket.org/mycompany/myrepo.git
              revision: c480097dfb84f12e897de3b1037dcecb7c86bc0e
              depth: 1
              usernameSecret:
                name: bitbucketserver-access
                key: username
              passwordSecret:
                name: bitbucketserver-access
                key: secret
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo-workflows -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-workflows
kubectl get events -n argo-workflows --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "getting `empty git-upload-pack given` from wf using git…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: The bug was caused by an upstream go-git commit that broke shallow clone fetching. Fix by upgrading the go-git dependency or pinning to a version before the breaking change.
codeSnippets:
artifacts:
          - name: argo-source
            path: /workspace
            git:
              repo: https://myuser@bitbucket.org/mycompany/myrepo.git
              revision: c480097dfb84f12e897de3b1037dcecb7c86bc0e
              depth: 1
              usernameSecret:
                name: bitbucketserver-access
                key: username
              passwordSecret:
                name: bitbucketserver-access
                key: secret

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with argo-workflows installed or the issue environment reproducible.
